title: pastinaca sativa subsp. pratensis
intro: Pastinaca sativa subsp. pratensis, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial plant of the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of wild parsnip include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Wild parsnip can be prepared as an herbal remedy through various methods, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
